title: Altered processing of self-produced sensations in psychosis at cortical and spinal levels.

assets.abstract: Psychosis is often characterized by disturbances in the sense of self, with patients frequently misattributing self-produced sensations to external sources. While somatic hallucinations and misperceptions are common, the underlying disruptions in basic bodily self-processing remain unclear. We aimed to investigate processing of self-evoked sensations, including touch and interoception, in psychosis using a multimodal, multi-method approach. This case-control-study included a total of 70 particip
